rationale: The subject home (centered on the orange reticle, bounded by the orange dashed box) appears to be under active construction. The bounding box measures approximately 255 × 268 pixels (54.1 ft × 56.8 ft), yielding a plan-view bounding area of ~3,074 sq ft. The roof is a complex hip style with multiple planes visible; the upper-left portion shows a completed darker hip section with discernible ridge/hip lines, while the lower-central area shows lighter exposed decking consistent with active framing. A fill factor of ~78% was applied to account for the hip corners tapering inward. No attached garage or separate structure is clearly connected within the box. Ridge (~2 segments, ~35 ft), hip lines (~6 edges from peaks to corners, ~95 ft total), valleys (~2 inward junctions, ~18 ft), no rakes (hip roof), and perimeter eaves (~155 ft) were estimated by tracing visible roof-plane edges in pixel coordinates and converting at 0.212 ft/px. Confidence is reduced due to the construction state partially obscuring roof structure and the unusual orientation of the home relative to north.
